content_gaps: Most pages conflate the 'evil eye' (the curse) with the 'Nazar' (the protective amulet). A dedicated section clarifying this distinction, as one article does, would be a significant value-add.

There is very little discussion about other significant eye symbols in jewelry beyond the Nazar/Evil Eye. A section covering the Eye of Horus, Lover's Eye, or other cultural eye symbols would provide more comprehensive coverage.

The topic of what it means when the jewelry breaks is a common user question but is only briefly mentioned in most articles. A detailed section on this specific event (breaking, falling off, etc.) and its significance would address a clear user concern.

While the history is mentioned, the specific evolution from ancient Mesopotamia, Egypt, and Greece to modern jewelry trends is often glossed over. Detailing the journey of the symbol through different civilizations and its adoption into modern fashion would add depth.

Practical advice on how to choose an eye necklace is largely missing. Content discussing materials (gold, silver, glass), styles (pendants, beads), and how to integrate them with other jewelry would be commercially valuable and answer latent user questions.
